summary_text: - **High risk**: The original worktree at `/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public` is heavily dirty (many tracked + untracked changes), so a routine `git add .` / `git commit -a` can accidentally ship unrelated runtime/CI/docs changes. - **High risk**: Sensitive operational files are already modified in that dirty tree, including [fugue-orchestration-gate.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml), [fugue-watchdog.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-watchdog.yml), and [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md), which increases blast radius if you commit from there. - **Medium risk**: The clean sibling worktree at `../fugue-orchestrator-clean` is on **detached HEAD** at `4b48069ae`, so future commits there are easier to misplace unless you anchor them to a named branch. **Recommendation (safest next executable action)** Create a named branch in the clean sibling worktree and do all future commits there (never from the dirty original tree). Suggested branch name: `safe/clean-main-followup` ```bash git -C ../fugue-orchestrator-clean switch -c safe/clean-main-followup ``` After this, treat `/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public` as “do not commit” until you intentionally reconcile it.
